  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "What should be the primary emphasis: (A) argument and claim solidity (logic plus claim–evidence alignment), (B) language polish/clarity, or (C) section-level structure/narrative? Please reply with A, B, or C (or a priority order, e.g., A > B).",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "“Make sure this is solid” most directly maps to verifying the internal argument chain and consistency of claims within the passage.",
    "R1.3": "Assessing solidity also involves checking whether assertive findings (e.g., perfect commitment preservation, comparative rankings) are supported by the reported results.",
    "RX.1": "Clear disclosure of exclusions and open questions should be maintained to accurately represent uncertainty and limits of validity.",
    "RX.2": "Overclaim avoidance is critical so conclusions do not extend beyond what the evaluation and stated assumptions justify.",
    "RX.3": "Scope adherence ensures the effort stays on assessing solidity rather than broad rewriting or adding new content.",
    "RX.4": "Downstream-harm avoidance matters because overconfident or imprecise claims about V2G control strategies could mislead stakeholders.",
    "RX.5": "Provenance and traceability require linking assessed claims back to their precise textual statements and cited evidence/sections."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: What should be the primary emphasis: (A) argument and claim solidity (logic plus claim–evidence alignment), (B) language polish/clarity, or (C) section-level structure/narrative? Please reply with A, B, or C (or a priority order, e.g., A > B).

rationale.R1.2: “Make sure this is solid” most directly maps to verifying the internal argument chain and consistency of claims within the passage.
rationale.R1.3: Assessing solidity also involves checking whether assertive findings (e.g., perfect commitment preservation, comparative rankings) are supported by the reported results.
rationale.RX.1: Clear disclosure of exclusions and open questions should be maintained to accurately represent uncertainty and limits of validity.
rationale.RX.2: Overclaim avoidance is critical so conclusions do not extend beyond what the evaluation and stated assumptions justify.
rationale.RX.3: Scope adherence ensures the effort stays on assessing solidity rather than broad rewriting or adding new content.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream-harm avoidance matters because overconfident or imprecise claims about V2G control strategies could mislead stakeholders.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability require linking assessed claims back to their precise textual statements and cited evidence/sections.
